channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: Break Through Your Limits with Dragon Ball Z The Board Game
item.content:encoded:
The world of the iconic Dragon Ball is getting a board game adaptation. Pop Art Games and Lynnvander Studios has announced Dragon Ball Z The Board Game, a campaign-driven experience that takes players through some of the series' most iconic battles.

Dragonball Z The Board Game Announcement

According to an official press release, Dragon Ball Z The Board Game is a cooperative experience designed for 1-4 players. Each campaign of the game is broken up into Episodes where players prepare to battle against several iconic villains from the series' vast history. Alternatively the game's different Episodes can be played as a standalone experience.

These episodes are played out in several intense turns that combine strategic movement, power-ups, and combat. Players will need to keep track of their Power Level, which determines how many actions they get in combat, and various combat Techniques represented by rolling dice. Players will need to manage their Power Level and Stress to gain strong Forms, master Techniques, and defend Earth.

The ultimate goal of the game is all players must work together to defeat all enemies on the board before they collect all seven Dragon Balls or before time runs out.

“There’s an incredible amount of gameplay for Dragon Ball Z fans to enjoy,” said Tommy Gofton of Lynnvander Studios. “The episodic delivery and battle sequencing really connects the anime series to the tabletop for an authentic gaming experience fans will enjoy for years to come.”

The press release is light on what exact parts of the source material Dragon Ball Z The Board Game will be directly adapting. The box art features characters from The Saiyan Saga, which aligns with the goal of the bad guys. Promo art also shows villains from the Buu Saga: Majin Buu, Super Buu, Majin Vegeta, and Babidi.

There's an equally strong possibility other arcs from the series might be added to the game via stretch goals. Yes, Dragon Ball Z The Board Game will be arriving on Kickstarter, allowing die hard fans to potentially get exclusive bonus materials through the campaign. Given the pop culture ubiquity of the series created by the late Akira Toriyama, there is a lot to pull from.

The Kickstarter campaign for the game will go live on June 9.

Ever since he was small, Tyler Chancey has had a deep, abiding love for video games and a tendency to think and overanalyze everything he enjoyed. This… More about Tyler



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/news/break-through-your-limits-with-dragon-ball-z-board-game
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Bushido Cold Blood Themed Warband Review - Join The Cult
item.content:encoded:
Some things are meant to remain forgotten, especially sealed temples to the Orochi, the Great Coiled One, and opening them always ends badly for everyone, even those who open them. In this article, we take a look at the new Ito Clan Cold Blood themed warband box for Bushido.

We've been covering Bushido for years, so if you'd like to learn more about the game, check out our guide to Bushido Risen Sun, our review of the Bushido Risen Sun 2-player starter set, and our "Start Collecting" guide to Bushido, which includes an interview with the game's developer.

Thematically Themed

When selecting a warband in Bushido, you must first choose a faction from the 15 available to select your fighters from. Each faction also has access to theme cards, which offer special abilities but restrict the units that can be included in the warband. For example, the Ito Clan is a faction for Bushido, and Cold Blood is a theme card that restricts you to Acolyte, Asura, Hebimiman, and Snake fighters, but gives them the ability to exchange a Blood of Orochi token, which a lot of Ito Clan generate and can be used to poison weapons, into a Virtue token instead. Virtue tokens can then be spent to gain ki, stop combat pools from being reduced, or to move after an enemy effect moves you.

Some Things Are Better Left Unfound

The Cold Blood themed warband is a mix of snake creatures, specifically those awoken by Ayako, and the Acolytes that support them. They have low melee pools across the warband, but they can put a lot of poison counters on enemies to wear them down. They don't hit hard, but can move fast, so you need to be wary of aggressive factions trying to overpower them and let your poison counters do the work.

Hissss

The Ito Clan, Cold Blood themed warband box, contains 7 models. These are:

  • Zhin-Sa - Zhin generates 2 Blood of Orochi tokens each turn, and also gains additional ki for each Blood of Orochi token they are allocated. This then powers their Fire Venom Seal ki feat, which can be powered between 1 and 3 ki, to give enemy models within 6 inches a poison marker equal to the ki spent and a spirit block marker to stop them from generating ki for a turn. It can only be used if they have a poison marker already,  but with the amount of poison output this warband has, that won't be a problem.
  • Nyjak-Ra - Nyjak is a ranged and melee threat for Cold Blood. They have a poison spit attack that dishes out poison tokens instead of damage. They can also use their Ophidian Coil ki feat that allows them to move into base-to-base contact with their target and give them a held marker if they win an opposed move roll, for which they have move 5, which can be boosted. Then during the end phase, any immobilised or held enemies in base-to-base contact with them gain a Poison 2 marker.
  • Kaman-Jol - Kaman also has a spit attack that drains ki, or gives poison tokens if they have no ki left. Enemy models entering base-to-base contact with them are also at risk of gaining stunned markers, which reduce their melee pool, with Kaman's Reputation ki feat.
  • Prak Vithara - Prak is 1 of 4 Acolytes in the warband, and she dishes out a free poison token to any enemies who enter base-to-base contact with her. She can use her Serpent's Nest, once per game ki feat to move 3 other Acoyltes within 4 inches up to their move value to push them up the board, as well as give Acyolytes who share a profile card the Group ability with her Strike as One ki feat.
  • Chey Kaliyan - Chey can only be included when using the Cold Blood theme and has the unique ability that gives any friendly models given exactly 1 Blood of Orochi token another for free, really boosting their ability to poison their weapons and letting you focus on pumping Orochi tokens onto Zhin. Chey also has a boostable ki feat, up to 3 ki, that can give poison 1 markers up to the ki spent to an enemy within 8 inches.
  • Muoy & Seang -  Muoy and Seang are cheap disposable Acolytes that can be moved quickly up the board by Prak. They have an Underhanded ki feat that lets them give out poison markers to enemies that have melee assist penalties against them. They also have the Sacrifice trait, which lets them be removed from the game, to stop a damage roll against a friendly Asura or Acolyte within 3 inches of them, as long as they're not in base-to-base contact with an enemy or exhausted.

Cult Recruitment

Releasing alongside the Cold Blood theme is the most powerful Hebimiman, who also has the Asura keyword. Kho-Ular is a literal terror, with Lightning Reflexes so they always attack first, and the unique effect that strips Lightning Reflexes from any enemy Asura models they are in base-to-base contact with. They also have Vengeance Asura, which gives them Brutal +1 and +1 melee strength against other Asura, so it's safe to assume they don't get along with them.

Kho-Ular generates 3 ki a turn, which they can use on their 3 available ki feats. Choke Hold lets them gain Grapple Attack and Grapple Defence, and if they successfully use one, they automatically deal a wound to their target. They also already have Combo Attack, Sweep Attack, and Sidestep Defence, and a unique effect that lets them apply both a special attack and defence during the same attack, where you are usually limited to only 1. They also have the Proficiency ki feat, which gives them Feint and Dodge until the end of the activation, but the ki feat they will use the most is Ophidian Dance, which lets them use their boostable movement of 5, in place of another stat for that test to really overwhelm in melee or opposed ki feats.

What Are Our Final Thoughts On The Cold Blood Themed Warband?

Cold Blood has an awesome backstory, with the Acolytes releasing monstrous creatures on the world, that may serve their purpose for a while, but probably won't want to serve anyone in the future. They can dish out insane amounts of poison markers and have some great combo attacks, as well as being snake-like fast and slippery to pin down, but not traditionally strong. Kho-Ular is beautifully monstrous in combat, while pricey for it. The aesthetic of their miniatures captures this dark theme incredibly well.

The copy of Bushido Cold Blood Themed Warband used to produce this review was provided by GCT Studios.

Adam is a Tabletop Specialist for TechRaptor. He started writing for TechRaptor in 2017 and took over as Tabletop Editor in 2019 and has since stood down… More about Adam



https://techraptor.net/tabletop/reviews/bushido-cold-blood-themed-warband-review-join-cult
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
